title: A History of Violence
logline: A mild-mannered small-town diner owner becomes a local hero after killing two criminals in self-defense, but his violent past catches up with him when mobsters arrive claiming he's someone else entirely.
antagonistOrConflict: Tom's violent past embodied by mobster Torrino and his brother Richie, forcing him to confront the identity he abandoned
tone: Tense, intimate, morally complex — domestic tranquility shattered by eruptions of sudden violence
themes: Violence as both protection and corruption
The cost of secrets on family

actBreaks.description: Tom kills Leland and Billy at the diner, becoming a local hero and triggering the arrival of his past.

validation.ruling: Scene #33 shows children playing with toy weapons on a Saturday afternoon—no plot twist or irreversible change occurs. Scene #39 (p31-34, 29%) ends Act 1: two killers attack the diner, Tom brutally kills both in self-defense, and this violent eruption fundamentally transforms the story from quiet small-town life to a thriller about Tom's hidden past, forcing him into new conflict he cannot escape.

actBreaks.description: Tom kills Torrino and his men at the McKenna house, fully exposing his identity as Joey Cusack to his family.

validation.ruling: Scene #83 (p78, 73%) ends Act 2 with the aftermath of the climactic shootout: Torrino lies dead, Tom is wounded, Jack has killed to save his father, and Edie looks at Tom with conflicting emotions while sirens approach—this irreversible moment where the family has been shattered by violence and truth marks the fundamental shift into the final act of reckoning and resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a pressure-cooker thriller built on a devastating irony: the act that makes Tom a hero is the same act that destroys him. The structure is deceptively simple — a linear three-act progression — but its power comes from how it weaponizes domesticity. Act One establishes an almost aggressively normal family life (breakfast scenes, baseball practice, tender marital moments) before the diner killing detonates everything. Act Two is a slow-motion implosion as Tom's two identities collide, with Torrino's presence forcing increasingly desperate lies. The mall confrontation (scene 61-66) is a masterclass in sustained dread. Act Three strips away all pretense: Tom becomes Joey, travels to Philadelphia, and confronts his brother Richie in a final reckoning that's as much about family as it is about violence. The script's genius is that it never lets you forget what's at stake — not Tom's life, but his family's ability to ever trust him again.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately bifurcated. The opening (scenes 1-2) is a slow-burn horror sequence establishing Leland and Billy's casual brutality. Then the script downshifts into domestic rhythms for 30 pages before the diner attack (scene 33) explodes at the exact structural midpoint of Act One. Act Two sustains tension through stillness — long dialogue scenes where subtext does the heavy lifting (the hospital room confrontation in scenes 40-42, the living room arguments in scenes 73-79). The final act accelerates: Philadelphia sequence (scenes 103-107) is rapid-fire, then the beach house confrontation (scenes 110-115) slows to a crawl for maximum dread. The script ends on profound quiet — scene 119 in the kitchen, no dialogue, just a family trying to figure out if they can still be a family.

incitingIncident.description: Judy spots Bobby climbing out of his car; she and Jack get up and walk the opposite direction to avoid him.

validation.ruling: Scene #33 is already designated as Act Break 1 and shows mundane Saturday afternoon activity with no story disruption. Scene #39 (p31-34, 29%) is the true inciting incident: two violent criminals attack Tom's diner, Tom kills them both in brutal self-defense, and this irreversible act shatters his ordinary world and establishes the central conflict that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Tom stands outside watching Torrino's black sedan as it slowly pulls away down the street and turns the corner.

validation.ruling: Scene #61 shows Tom passively watching a sedan drive away with no character shift or revelation. Scene #71 (p61-66, 57%) is the true midpoint: Tom confesses his hidden past as Joey Cusack to Edie in the bathroom, revealing he killed his abusive father and fled Philadelphia—this confession is the point of no return that shifts Tom from reactive denial to proactive truth-telling and fundamentally transforms his relationship with Edie.

climax.description: Tom tackles Charlie and they fight brutally on the ground, Tom bites Charlie's hand until blood spurts and punches his throat repeatedly until Charlie chokes and dies, then a bullet tears through Tom's shoulder and Torrino stands over him with a cocked gun pointed at his head.

validation.ruling: Scene #115 is the confrontation with Richie but occurs in the basement setup phase. Scene #80 (p76, 71%) is the true climax: Tom fights Charlie to the death in brutal hand-to-hand combat, then Torrino shoots Tom and stands over him ready to execute him—this is the peak confrontation where Tom faces the ultimate antagonist at maximum stakes, resolving the main story question of whether his past will destroy him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Billy walks into a motel room and sees blood splattered on the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL OFFICE
scenes.summary: Billy walks to the motel office while Leland waits in the car; Leland mentions there was trouble with the maid but everything's fine now.

scenes.heading: INT. MCKENNAHOUSE - SARAH'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Tom McKenna wakes at the sound of a scream and races to the door in the dark.

scenes.heading: INT. MCKENNAHOUSE - TOM & EDIE'S BEDROOM- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Billy pulls a gun on a little girl who emerges from the bathroom after he's taken water bottles; the motel clerk and maid lie dead; the scene cuts to Tom and Edie discussing their daughter Sarah's nightmare about shadow monsters.

scenes.heading: INT. HANK QUIGLEY'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Hank Quigley lies in bed in his small, dilapidated house.

scenes.heading: EXT. HANK QUIGLEY'S HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks over the McKenna farmhouse surrounded by cornfields.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Edie comforts Sarah while Tom reassures his son Jack, who is embarrassed by his father's affection.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Jack enters the kitchen for breakfast; Edie notices Tom's half-smile as he thinks about their children.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S STATION WAGON
scenes.summary: Tom showers, revealing scars across his back for the first time.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE - ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The McKenna family drives toward town discussing Jack's upcoming baseball game and math test while Tom and Edie reflect on not feeling like real grown-ups yet.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE - CENTER
scenes.summary: Tom gets out of the car and Edie drives away as Tom walks toward town.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE OUTSKIRTS
scenes.summary: Tom and Edie flirt about going to a drive-in and making out; they look at each other with intensity, still very much in love.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom approaches his diner, scrapes a smudge off the window, and pauses when he sees something.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER - DAY
scenes.summary: Jared and other high school kids in a car with loud rap music greet Tom; Jared tells him Charlotte will be late; inside the diner, Mick and Pat discuss a psychiatrist while Tom listens.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom greets a middle-aged woman entering the diner and spots Hank Quigley standing outside looking in thoughtfully.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom invites Hank inside; Hank asks Tom to speak with Edie about letting him see his grandchildren before they're not children anymore, wanting to make up for his past; Tom agrees to bring it up with Edie.

scenes.heading: INT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - BASEBALL FIELD - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack catches a fly ball in right field, ending the game with the Reds winning; his teammates cheer him while Bobby storms off furious.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - BASEBALL FIELD - LATER
scenes.summary: Bobby pushes Jack against his locker and threatens him, calling him chickenshit.

scenes.heading: INT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - BOY'S LOCKER ROOM
scenes.summary: Jack agrees with Bobby's insults, defusing the confrontation; Bobby pushes him against the locker again and storms off while other kids laugh.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH
scenes.summary: Alex compliments Jack on how he handled Bobby by agreeing with him; they walk toward school as rain begins.

scenes.heading: INT. LELAND AND BILLY'S CAR
scenes.summary: An attractive young woman leans over her broken-down car on the side of the road looking frustrated.

scenes.heading: EXT. TWO LANE BLACKTOP
scenes.summary: Leland and Billy drive past the stranded woman; Billy insists they go back despite Leland's reluctance.

scenes.heading: INT. LELAND AND BILLY'S CAR
scenes.summary: Leland and Billy drive along listening to Johnny Cash as light rain falls.

scenes.heading: EXT. TWO LANE BLACKTOP - DUSK
scenes.summary: Billy examines the woman's carburetor and tells her to come closer; she nervously mentions her husband is expecting her home.

scenes.heading: EXT. TWO LANE BLACKTOP
scenes.summary: The young woman drives away smiling, wearing a Johnny Cash t-shirt with an image of him flipping the bird.

scenes.heading: INT. ATTRACTIVE YOUNGWOMAN'SCAR
scenes.summary: Leland asks Billy if he's done; Billy agrees to no more stops tonight.

scenes.heading: INT. LELAND AND BILLY'S CAR - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Billy tells the woman to take off her shirt because she shouldn't be driving in her condition; he reaches into his back pocket with gleaming eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S STATION WAGON
scenes.summary: Tom and Edie discuss never getting to be teenagers together and plan to go somewhere; Tom mentions the cannonball.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER - DUSK
scenes.summary: Edie picks up Tom outside the diner at dusk as the rain lets up.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Edie comes out of the bathroom in her nightgown and kisses Tom warmly on the mouth.

scenes.heading: EXT. POND - TWENTY SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Edie and Tom leap into the car loosely dressed and peel out.

scenes.heading: EXT. POND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tom and Edie swim and kiss at the pond, swatting mosquitoes; Tom tells Edie he's the luckiest man alive and she recalls the moment she knew he loved her.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom tells Edie that her father Hank came by the diner wanting to see her and meet the kids; Edie tenses up immediately at this news.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank Quigley's reappearance introduces family conflict that will complicate the domestic peace.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Tom walks down the street on Saturday afternoon as kids with toy superhero weapons race past him playing.

scenes.heading: INT. BOBBY'S CAR
scenes.summary: Edie tells Tom he's the best man she's ever known and turns on the bedside lamp; Tom smiles and rolls over to sleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jack and Judy sit in Bobby's car at the town square passing a joint, discussing what kids did for fun a hundred years ago and whether this is as good as it gets.

scenes.heading: INT. LELAND AND BILLY'S TRUCK
scenes.summary: Leland and Billy drive in their truck discussing stopping for pie; Billy says he's sick of their situation.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Bobby nearly drives into another car and flips them off; Billy gives Bobby a cold glare that scares him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWNSQUARE
scenes.summary: Judy spots Bobby climbing out of his car; she and Jack get up and walk the opposite direction to avoid him.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Leland and Billy rob the diner at gunpoint; Tom offers them the money and tells Charlotte she can leave, but Billy blocks the door; Leland pulls a gun on Tom while Billy points his gun at Charlotte as Lisa screams.
scenes.turningPointNote: The robbery erupts into violence, forcing Tom to act and revealing his lethal capabilities.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER - ON TV - TOM'S POV
scenes.summary: A TV reporter stands in front of the shattered diner window describing how Tom McKenna fought off two wanted criminals; the broadcast shows Tom being loaded into an ambulance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom lies in a hospital bed watching TV coverage of the incident; Leland lies dead on the floor and Billy lies dead outside on the sidewalk; paramedics treat Tom's injuries as Edie bursts through to hug him.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA HOUSE - DUSK
scenes.summary: A TV pundit on The Runyan Report calls Tom McKenna an American hero for his actions at the diner.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL WAITING AREA
scenes.summary: Tom is wheeled out of the hospital to find dozens of locals waiting to congratulate him; Jack carries Sarah over and Tom kisses his son on the forehead.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA BOUSE - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Tom arrives home and sits in his armchair; Jack tells him reporters just want an interview because he's a hero, but Tom insists what he did was stupid and lucky.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: A perky reporter tries to interview Tom outside his house; he politely declines and walks inside to be with his family.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Edie comes to Tom in her nightgown and puts her arms around him in their bedroom.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Tom looks out the window and sees a dark sedan parked across the field by the road with its lights on dim.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mysterious surveillance car signals that Tom's past is catching up to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE
scenes.summary: Tom and Edie discuss the reporters camped outside; Edie reassures Tom she won't let this eat him alive and kisses him to show how they treat good men.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S STATION WAGON
scenes.summary: Edie drives Tom to work hidden under sheets in the back to avoid reporters; she drops him off on the side of the road.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE OUTSKIRTS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Tom returns to his diner for the first time since the shooting, where Mick hugs him and Charlotte greets him, and Tom picks up a piece of glass from the floor before throwing it in the trash.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Outside the diner, a kid playing pretend gunfight runs into Tom while imitating shooting, and Tom helps him up as the disturbed kid looks at him in awe.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Three out-of-place men in suits—Aldo, Charlie, and scarred Giuseppe Torrino—enter the diner, ignoring the stares from locals, and look intently at Tom.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Torrino calls Tom 'Joey' while ordering coffee, Tom insists his name is Tom, and a huge crowd of locals swarms in to congratulate Tom as a hero while Torrino and his men sit watching.
scenes.turningPointNote: Torrino's arrival and insistence that Tom is 'Joey' introduces the central conflict of mistaken/hidden identity that will drive the rest of the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLEOUTSKIRTS
scenes.summary: Edie calls Sheriff Sam to report the three men, Torrino thanks Tom for the coffee calling him Joey again, Tom corrects him saying his name is Tom McKenna, and Sam warns the men to leave town.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: At a gas station, Sam warns Aldo to leave town, Aldo responds dismissively calling Sam 'officer' instead of sheriff, and they drive away as Sam watches troubled.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Sam visits Tom and Edie at their house and directly asks Tom if he's in witness protection, Tom denies it, and Edie becomes upset realizing the danger.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom reassures Edie that the men have the wrong guy and will leave, saying the attention will blow over and they'll return to being anonymous.

scenes.heading: INT. MCKENNA HOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tom and Edie discuss her father in bed, Edie says her father killed her mother and she wants nothing to do with him, then Tom has a nightmare where he shoots Leland but the body becomes Torrino laughing and pointing a gun at him.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom encounters Hank during an early morning walk and invites him inside the diner.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA'S DINER - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Tom stands outside watching Torrino's black sedan as it slowly pulls away down the street and turns the corner.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA'SDINER
scenes.summary: Torrino's sedan sits in the street with the engine running, and Tom freezes when he sees it.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Edie grabs the 12-gauge shotgun from the closet and races out of the room.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Tom runs desperately down the street on his injured foot while talking on his phone, telling Edie to get the shotgun because they're coming to the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE OUTSKIRTS
scenes.summary: Edie wakes to the phone ringing and answers to hear Tom's frantic warning that someone is coming to the house.

scenes.heading: INT. MCKENNA HOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Tom pulls out his cell phone and punches a number while running desperately on his injured foot.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE - STREET
scenes.summary: Tom runs through a cornfield, falls hard, staggers up and continues running until he reaches the house where Edie stands holding the shotgun, confused, and he collapses into her arms.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE OUTSKIRTS
scenes.summary: A shoe saleswoman helps Edie try on shoes while Sarah wanders the store bored.

scenes.heading: INT. TAYLORVILLE Ml\LL - SHOE STORE
scenes.summary: Edie and Sarah arrive at the mall, and Torrino's black sedan pulls into the parking lot behind them.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE MALL - DAY
scenes.summary: A crowd of locals swarms Tom at the diner, patting him and shaking his hand, and he's overwhelmed by the hero's welcome.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Sarah disappears from the shoe store, Edie panics and runs out still wearing the new shoes, and finds Sarah outside the toy store with Charlie and Torrino, who were watching her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Torrino's men directly threaten Tom's family by approaching his daughter, escalating from surveillance to active intimidation and raising the stakes to include his children's safety.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Torrino thanks Edie for her time and compliments Sarah, Edie furiously threatens to shoot him if he comes near her family again, and as she walks away with Sarah her terrified face cracks beneath her tough facade.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - LIVING ROOM- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Jack violently kicks and beats Bobby in a school hallway while possessed with rage, and a teacher pulls him off the bleeding victim.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Edie tells Tom about getting a restraining order against Torrino and reveals that Torrino believes Tom is someone named Joey.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNAHOUSE - TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Tom reassures crying Edie and upset Sarah that the bad men have the wrong person and everything will be okay, then sends Sarah upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Tom grabs the rifle and shotgun from the closet.

scenes.heading: INT. TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Hank arrives at the house wanting to apologize to Edie, Tom tries to convince him she might listen, and when Hank walks away Tom follows him outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Hank unlocks the diner and enters, then a figure approaches him in the early morning—it's Hank arriving to see Tom.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Hank tells Edie he wants to apologize and that good can come from bad, Edie coldly asks what he wants to say, Hank can't speak and stumbles out, then a bullet slams into him from outside and he falls dead in the doorway.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank's sudden murder at the doorway marks the eruption of lethal violence into Tom's home, shattering any remaining illusion of safety and forcing the final confrontation.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNAHOUSE
scenes.summary: Tom tackles Charlie and they fight brutally on the ground, Tom bites Charlie's hand until blood spurts and punches his throat repeatedly until Charlie chokes and dies, then a bullet tears through Tom's shoulder and Torrino stands over him with a cocked gun pointed at his head.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Edie curls on the floor screaming as bullets slam into the walls and furniture around her.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: A paramedic bandages Tom's shoulder while Sam questions him, Edie holds dazed Sarah, and Jack stands in the distance staring at where Torrino's body lay as firemen put out the field fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA HOUSE - LATER
scenes.summary: Jack stands frozen holding the shotgun with a dazed look, Tom takes the shotgun from him, Torrino's chest explodes from a shotgun blast and he falls dead, Edie emerges with Sarah and rushes to hug Jack, then Tom approaches and takes his family in his arms.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jack kills Torrino to save his father, forcing the son to commit the same violence that has defined Tom's hidden past and irrevocably changing the family dynamic.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tom lies on a stretcher and Edie climbs into the ambulance beside him, holding his hand but not looking at him with a distant expression on her face.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE
scenes.summary: Sam offers to take the kids so Edie can ride with Tom, and Tom confesses to Edie that he and his friend Richie planned to rob Torrino's cheese shop for revenge after Torrino killed Tom's brother Steve, intending to make it look like a robbery.

scenes.heading: INT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Edie tells Tom she needs to think and walks out, Tom touches her arm and says Joey's dead, and she leaves.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - BASEBALL FIELD - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom gets into a cab which slowly pulls away.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack sits in class looking out the window while a teacher drones on about geography.

scenes.heading: INT. TAYLORVILLE HIGH - CLASSROOM
scenes.summary: An attendant helps Tom into a wheelchair and wheels him to the door.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jack confronts Tom asking what to call him now and saying Tom is the monster in Sarah's closet, and Tom doesn't answer his son's angry questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom's taxi passes the diner where Charlotte waits on customers through the window, business as usual.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA'S DINER
scenes.summary: Tom's taxi rolls through town as he looks out the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAYLORVILLE - STREET
scenes.summary: Sam visits Tom at home, and Tom brings him lemonade in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Sam arrives at the McKenna house and Tom invites him in for lemonade.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Tom walks outside and loads burnt corn husks and metal debris from the field into a wheelbarrow.

scenes.heading: INT. TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Tom stands in the bedroom doorway looking around—nothing has changed but nothing feels the same.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE - SARAH'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Tom sits on Sarah's bed, picks up one of her dolls, gently puts it back, and looks out the window.

scenes.heading: INT. McKENNA HOUSE
scenes.summary: Sam tells Tom that Torrino was a professional who wouldn't act without certainty he had the right man, Edie arrives home with Sarah, Tom hugs his daughter and he and Edie kiss perfunctorily, and Jack tells Tom he can't tell him anything and walks away upset.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sam's revelation that Torrino was certain of Tom's identity, combined with the family's cold reception, confirms that Tom's past is undeniable and his family relationships are fundamentally damaged.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN TRACKS - DUSK
scenes.summary: Edie tells Tom she doesn't know who he is anymore—Tom McKenna or Joey—and says they must live with the lie until it becomes truth, pretending to be Tom and Edie until maybe someday it feels real again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Edie's conditional acceptance—choosing to live a conscious lie rather than embrace or reject the truth—establishes the new fractured foundation of their marriage and the film's resolution.

scenes.heading: INT. TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Edie sleeps while Tom lies awake staring at the ceiling uncomfortable in his own bed, then a phone rings in the darkness and a man's voice says 'Help me, Joey.'
scenes.turningPointNote: The mysterious call for 'Joey' suggests Tom's past is not fully resolved and another threat or obligation from his former life is emerging, denying him the fragile peace he sought.

scenes.heading: INT. TOM AND EDIE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A priest lowers a casket into an open grave while Edie stands watching with Sarah, Tom alongside them, and a gravedigger present.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETARY - DUSK
scenes.summary: Tom tells Edie that Richie is still alive and has been captured by Torrino's organization, revealing the conflict isn't over despite Torrino's death, while Edie insists she doesn't want to hear it and Jack receives comfort from Judy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tom learns Richie has been captured, forcing him to confront his past again and setting up the final confrontation

scenes.heading: INT. CAFE DES ARTISTES
scenes.summary: Joey walks up to a corner restaurant in the middle of the city, looks at the sign outside, and walks in.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAFE DES ARTISTES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joey sits in the shadows of the Art Museum steps at night observing the brightly lit city skyline, smiles faintly at two souped-up cars racing past, then stands and heads down to his truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. PHILADELPHIA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tom opens the barn doors behind the McKenna house, goes inside, and drives out in a battered pickup truck that swerves around the station wagon and disappears down the road.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNAHOUSE
scenes.summary: Edie drops onto the bed in shock after Tom walks out without looking back, tries to turn on the lamp which flickers, then in a flash of rage smashes it against the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. TRACKAND TURF - LATER
scenes.summary: Joey meets a man in a booth at Track and Turf, asks about buying semi-automatic weapons with stopping power and ammunition, then inquires about Murphy's Tavern which the maitre'd says was shut down six years ago for serving minors.

scenes.heading: INT. TORRINO'S CHEESE SHOP
scenes.summary: Joey stands across the street in the shadows looking at Torrino's old cheese shop, pulls a gun from his jacket, cocks it, and walks determinedly towards the store.

scenes.heading: EXT. TORRINO'S CHEESE SHOP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A large man in a football jersey stands by the front door of Torrino's cheese shop keeping watch when he hears a scuffling noise and whirls, producing a gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH HOUSE
scenes.summary: Joey sits in his truck at a beach house driveway sipping coffee from a mug, fully armed with crisscrossing underarm holsters, guns, and a large knife, then straps on a leg holster under his pants, puts on a jacket, and gets out.

scenes.heading: INT. JOEY'S TRUCK
scenes.summary: Joey's truck is pulled over by the side of the road as waves crash on the beach and the sun rises over the horizon.

scenes.heading: EXT. SURF CITY STREET - DAWN
scenes.summary: Joey asks an old man with a dead arm about Torrino and is told Torrino is gone, receives a piece of paper, and walks grimly out of the cheese shop while the old man returns to his work.

scenes.heading: INT. BEACH HOUSE
scenes.summary: Joey appears behind Thug #1 with a knife, clamps a hand over his mouth, and repeatedly stabs him in the back until he sags to the ground with blood staining his jacket, then turns to the house.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT - SMALL ROOM
scenes.summary: Joey cautiously descends into the dark basement, turns on the lights to find it empty, hears a voice calling his name from behind a door, and approaches it while blood-stained from the men he's killed.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT
scenes.summary: Joey enters to find Richie sitting tied to a chair, but when Joey leans down to untie him, Richie reveals his hands aren't actually tied and pulls a gun on Joey, revealing he orchestrated the entire trap and has been running Torrino's organization.
scenes.turningPointNote: The ultimate betrayal is revealed—Richie is the real enemy who set up the entire conflict to lure Joey back and eliminate him

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Tom's pickup pulls up to the McKenna house at dusk, he gets out and sees the station wagon parked in front and the field cleaned of propane tank remnants, then walks to the front door and opens it.

scenes.heading: EXT. McKENNA HOUSE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Joey walks to the beach battered and blood-soaked, hurls his revolvers into the sea, throws his bloody knife and holsters after them, rips off his bloody shirt, then sits on the sand staring at the horizon as the sun rises and water laps closer.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH HOUSE
scenes.summary: Richie threatens to harm Joey's family, Joey whips his head left causing Richie's gun to slide past and go off harmlessly, then Joey stabs Richie in the belly with a knife and calmly pulls out his own gun to point at Richie as Richie staggers back clutching the wound.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joey kills Richie, eliminating the final threat from his past and completing his violent reckoning

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: The scene heading indicates the kitchen but no content is provided.
